name: analyzing-patterns
description: Automatically activated when user asks to "find patterns in...", "identify repeated code...", "analyze the architecture...", "what design patterns are used...", or needs to understand code organization, recurring structures, or architectural decisions
capabilities: Identify Gang of Four and modern design patterns in code
Recognize architectural patterns like MVC, layered, and microservices
Detect code repetition and duplication across files
Extract naming conventions and organization strategies
author: C0ntr0lledCha0s
securitySummary: This is a read-only pattern analysis skill. The static analyzer flagged 369 issues, but all findings are FALSE POSITIVES caused by misinterpretation of markdown documentation examples as executable code. The skill only reads files from user-specified directories and outputs pattern analysis using regex matching. No network access, no credential handling, no dangerous operations.
valueStatement: Understanding code structure and patterns helps developers navigate unfamiliar codebases faster. This skill identifies design patterns, architectural styles, and recurring code structures to provide insights into how software is organized.
limitations: Pattern detection uses signature matching; may miss custom implementations
Cannot execute code or perform runtime analysis
Analysis quality depends on code clarity and naming conventions

useCases.title: Understand project structure
useCases.description: Quickly grasp how a new codebase is organized by identifying architectural patterns and design choices.

useCases.title: Find code duplication
useCases.description: Identify repeated code blocks and patterns that could be extracted into shared utilities.

useCases.title: Assess design quality
useCases.description: Evaluate whether patterns are correctly implemented and appropriate for the problem domain.

promptTemplates.title: Find design patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: What design patterns are used in this codebase? Look for Factory, Singleton, Observer, and Strategy patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Analyze architecture
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the application architecture. What pattern is used? Describe the directory structure and layer organization.

promptTemplates.title: Find duplication
promptTemplates.prompt: Find duplicate or similar code blocks in this codebase. List the files and explain what can be refactored.

promptTemplates.title: Anti-pattern check
promptTemplates.prompt: Identify anti-patterns in this codebase. Look for God Objects, magic numbers, and tight coupling.

bestPractices: Provide file paths and line numbers for all pattern discoveries
Distinguish between patterns used correctly versus anti-patterns
Suggest refactoring only when it improves maintainability
antiPatterns: Recommending pattern application where simpler code suffices
Missing context-specific adaptations of standard patterns
Focusing on pattern names rather than code quality impact

faq.question: What languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: Supports TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Java, Go, and Rust with pattern signatures for each.

faq.question: How accurate is pattern detection?
faq.answer: Detection uses code signatures; it may miss unconventional implementations but catches standard patterns.

faq.question: Can this skill modify my code?
faq.answer: No. The skill only reads files and provides analysis. It does not write or modify any code.

faq.question: Does this skill send data externally?
faq.answer: No. All analysis happens locally. No network calls are made during pattern detection.

faq.question: Why were some patterns not detected?
faq.answer: Patterns may not be detected if they use non-standard naming, are implemented without typical signatures, or span multiple files in unusual ways.

faq.question: How is this different from a linter?
faq.answer: Linters check code style and errors. This skill identifies architectural patterns and design structures that require semantic understanding.
